s-1.ptxt: INSPECT PACK CLEARANCE OF FORWARD CLUTCH

s2.ptxt: Install the forward clutch on the oil pump.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the oil seal ring of the oil pump.

s2.ptxt: Using a dial indicator, measure the forward clutch pack clearance while applying and releasing compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi).
If the clearance is not within the standard, inspect the discs, plates and flange.
If the piston stroke is less than the minimum, parts may have been assembled incorrectly. Check and reassemble again.

item.ptxt: As the opening is large, cover it with a piece of cloth or shop rag to prevent the compressed air from being released.
There are 5 different thicknesses of flanges available.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FORWARD MULTIPLE DISC CLUTCH DISC

s2.ptxt: Using a screwdriver, remove the snap ring.
Remove the flange, 5 discs and 5 plates from the input shaft assembly.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE FORWARD CLUTCH RETURN SPRING SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Place SST on the spring retainer and compress the return spring with a press.
Using a snap ring expander, remove the snap ring.

item.ptxt: Stop the press when the spring seat is lowered to the place 1 to 2 mm (0.039 to 0.078 in.) from the snap ring groove. This prevents the spring seat from bearing deformed.
Do not expand the snap ring excessively.

s2.ptxt: Remove the clutch balancer from the input shaft.
Remove the forward clutch return spring from the input shaft.
Place the forward clutch drum onto the oil pump.
Holding the forward clutch piston by hand, apply compressed air (392 kPa, 4.0 kgf/cm2, 57 psi) to the oil pump to remove the forward clutch piston.

atten4.ptxt: When the piston cannot be removed as it is slanted, either blow the air again with the protruding side pushed, or remove the piston using needle nose pliers with the tips taped.

s-1.ptxt: REMOVE INPUT SHAFT OIL SEAL RING

s2.ptxt: Remove the input shaft oil seal ring from the input shaft.
